requirements.description: Public web ingress shall be exposed only through the approved CDN edge, with no alternate public entry path for application endpoints.
requirements.threshold: 100% of externally reachable production HTTP and HTTPS application endpoints resolve through the approved CDN; zero alternate public ingress endpoints are reachable in quarterly external reachability tests.
requirements.measurement_method: Network ingress configuration review and quarterly external reachability scanning of public endpoints.

requirements.description: All CDN-to-origin connections shall use encrypted transport.
requirements.threshold: 100% of production CDN-to-origin connections negotiate TLS 1.2 or higher; zero plaintext origin connections are observed in configuration review or transport validation tests.
requirements.measurement_method: Ingress proxy and edge configuration review plus transport security validation on origin connections.

requirements.description: Direct client access to origin infrastructure shall be blocked.
requirements.threshold: 0 direct client connections to the origin IP succeed from external probes across HTTP, HTTPS, and known application ports in quarterly perimeter tests.
requirements.measurement_method: External network probing and firewall or proxy rule inspection against origin IP exposure.

requirements.description: All application reads and writes shall enforce tenant-scoped isolation at the data layer, and cross-tenant access attempts shall be denied and audit logged.
requirements.threshold: 100% of cross-tenant read and write authorization tests fail at the database policy layer; 100% of denied cross-tenant attempts create an audit event with actor, tenant context, target, and timestamp.
requirements.measurement_method: PostgreSQL schema and policy inspection, automated authorization tests across tenant boundaries, and audit-log verification.

requirements.description: Delegated authority and role-based authorization changes shall be scope-bounded, time-bounded, revocable, and reflected promptly in authorization decisions.
requirements.threshold: 100% of delegated actions record both acting user and represented principal; 100% of out-of-scope or expired delegated actions are denied; newly issued authorization decisions reflect permission or role changes within 60 seconds of commit in 99.9% of trials.
requirements.measurement_method: Authorization integration tests covering grant, revoke, expiry, scope boundaries, and permission propagation timing.

requirements.description: Customer-uploaded documents and media shall be encrypted at rest, and all client-facing network traffic carrying authentication tokens, payment references, PII, or case content shall use modern TLS.
requirements.threshold: 100% of stored customer-uploaded documents and media are encrypted at rest with KMS-backed keys; 100% of client-facing sessions carrying sensitive content negotiate TLS 1.2 or higher; zero plaintext exceptions in monthly control review.
requirements.measurement_method: Storage encryption configuration inspection, object-sample verification, and transport security scans of client-facing endpoints.

requirements.description: Search, document retrieval, and entity-level views shall enforce record visibility based on authorized property, community, case, and role scope.
requirements.threshold: 100% of unauthorized retrieval attempts for documents and indexed search results are denied in authorization regression tests; zero unauthorized records appear in sampled search results across supported roles.
requirements.measurement_method: Access-control regression suite across search and retrieval endpoints plus seeded visibility test datasets.

requirements.description: Authenticated interactive read APIs shall meet low-latency response targets under normal operating load.
requirements.threshold: p95 server response time for authenticated interactive read operations is 500 ms or less over rolling 5-minute windows at normal operating load.
requirements.measurement_method: Server-side timing histograms with synthetic and production-load sampling, backed by SLO alerting.

requirements.description: AI-assisted intake normalization shall complete within a user-visible latency budget for typical homeowner submissions.
requirements.threshold: p95 end-user visible completion time is 15 seconds or less for intake submissions containing up to 10 attachments under normal operating load.
requirements.measurement_method: End-to-end synthetic intake benchmarks and production transaction timing for normalized submission flows.

requirements.description: Outbound messaging state shall be persisted promptly after provider delivery callbacks are received.
requirements.threshold: 95% of outbound email and SMS delivery-status updates are persisted within 2 minutes of provider callback receipt, and 99.9% within 10 minutes.
requirements.measurement_method: Callback-to-persistence latency instrumentation and periodic reconciliation of provider callback logs against stored message state.

requirements.description: Workflow-driven payment, messaging, provider-sync, and other multi-step operations shall be idempotent, monotonic, and recoverable from integration failures without losing the primary case record.
requirements.threshold: 100% of tested replayed operations with the same idempotency key produce a single business outcome; 100% of tested state transitions follow the defined monotonic path; 100% of simulated integration failures leave the primary case record intact and place the external action in retry or manual-review state.
requirements.measurement_method: Workflow engine behavior tests on repeated execution, failure-injection scenarios, and state-transition audit review.

requirements.description: Scheduling events and core transactional records shall be durably persisted and recoverable within the defined data-loss objective.
requirements.threshold: 100% of committed scheduling events are durably recorded before workflow completion acknowledgement; demonstrated recovery point objective for core transactional data is 15 minutes or less in disaster-recovery exercises.
requirements.measurement_method: Scheduling event persistence tests, workflow storage inspection, backup and restore validation, and quarterly disaster-recovery drills.

requirements.description: Core case management, portal access, and messaging workflows shall be restorable within the defined service recovery objective after a major outage.
requirements.threshold: Demonstrated recovery time objective is 4 hours or less for restoration of case management, portal access, and messaging workflows in quarterly disaster-recovery exercises.
requirements.measurement_method: Quarterly disaster-recovery drills with timed restoration checkpoints for the defined workflow set.

requirements.description: Mobile field operations shall remain usable in low-connectivity conditions through offline queuing and safe synchronization.
requirements.threshold: 100% of tested offline job updates, notes, photos, and status changes are queued locally when connectivity is unavailable and synchronize within 5 minutes of reconnect in 95% of degraded-connectivity test runs, with zero duplicate records or backward state transitions.
requirements.measurement_method: Native mobile offline and degraded-connectivity scenario tests covering queued operations and synchronization outcomes.

requirements.description: Board, resident, and homeowner portals shall conform to WCAG 2.1 AA for keyboard navigation, color contrast, and screen-reader support.
requirements.threshold: 100% of critical user journeys pass WCAG 2.1 AA automated checks with no Sev-1 accessibility defects, and all manual audit findings blocking keyboard-only or screen-reader completion are remediated before release.
requirements.measurement_method: Automated accessibility scanning plus manual keyboard and screen-reader audits on release candidates.

requirements.description: Case workflows, approvals, communications, admin actions, and payment state changes shall produce immutable audit history retained for long-term review.
requirements.threshold: 100% of case status changes, approvals, admin actions, workflow actions, communications, and payment state changes create immutable audit events; audit records are retained for at least 7 years; zero tested delete or update operations can alter finalized audit entries.
requirements.measurement_method: Workflow history inspection, audit-log integrity tests, retention-policy verification, and tamper-resistance testing.

requirements.description: Financial records shall maintain an auditable general ledger structure with currency-safe precision and full allocation traceability.
requirements.threshold: 100% of posted financial events create balanced ledger entries traceable to source business records; 100% of stored and computed externally visible monetary amounts use fixed-point decimal precision; 100% of payment allocations are reconstructable from immutable history.
requirements.measurement_method: Accounting data model inspection, ledger traceability tests, precision-validation tests, and financial audit sampling.

requirements.description: Configured statutory deadlines and required notices shall be computed, tracked, and evidenced so compliance obligations are not missed.
requirements.threshold: 100% of records subject to configured statutory deadlines or notice requirements receive a due date, status, and responsible party; 100% of required notices record issuance timestamp and delivery status; 0 overdue statutory items lack an alert in compliance regression tests.
requirements.measurement_method: Rules-engine validation against representative compliance scenarios, deadline-monitoring tests, and notice trail inspection.

requirements.description: Board voting workflows shall enforce configured eligibility, quorum, and outcome rules and preserve evidence of the final result.
requirements.threshold: 100% of voting regression scenarios enforce configured voter eligibility and quorum rules before a result is finalized; 100% of finalized vote outcomes retain voter responses, timestamps, and outcome calculation evidence.
requirements.measurement_method: Governance workflow rule tests using representative board voting scenarios and audit-trail verification.

requirements.description: Resale packet workflows shall produce complete, traceable records aligned to configured packet requirements and delivery obligations.
requirements.threshold: 100% of generated resale packets include all configured required artifacts before release; 100% of packet releases record issuer, timestamp, included artifacts, and recipient or delivery event.
requirements.measurement_method: Packet completeness validation against configured requirements and release-audit inspection.

requirements.description: Provider qualification and regulatory-compliance data used for matching or scheduling shall be current, provenance-tagged, and capable of blocking non-compliant work.
requirements.threshold: 100% of externally sourced qualification records include source provenance and last-verified timestamp before influencing matching recommendations; 100% of tested providers missing required active qualifications are blocked from schedulable appointment states; compliance alerts for expirations or non-compliant status changes are generated within 1 workflow run.
requirements.measurement_method: Qualification data inspection, matching and scheduling rule tests, and alert-generation workflow tests.

requirements.description: The platform shall expose operational telemetry and compliance evidence sufficient to detect performance regressions and audit control failures without impairing transactional workflows.
requirements.threshold: 100% of critical APIs, workflow executions, messaging callbacks, and compliance-alert jobs emit trace, metric, and error telemetry with correlated identifiers; reporting snapshot refresh jobs add no more than 5% latency overhead to transactional p95 response time during scheduled runs.
requirements.measurement_method: Telemetry coverage review, trace sampling, metrics dashboard inspection, and controlled load tests during reporting snapshot refreshes.

requirements.description: Scheduled appointments and related participant-facing times shall be stored and rendered with correct timezone semantics.
requirements.threshold: 100% of appointment records store timezone-aware timestamps; 100% of tested participant views render appointment time correctly for the participant profile timezone or property timezone across daylight-saving transitions.
requirements.measurement_method: Data-model inspection and automated timezone rendering tests across supported locales and daylight-saving boundaries.
